questions.text: Name the type of bond that forms between amino acids during protein synthesis.
Explain how the structure of haemoglobin is related to its function as an oxygen transport molecule.
questions.markScheme:
1. Quaternary structure with four polypeptide subunits (1)
2. Each subunit contains a haem group with an iron ion that binds to oxygen (1)
3. Cooperative binding means binding of first O2 changes shape making it easier for subsequent O2 molecules to bind (1)

questions.text: Describe and explain the effect of increasing temperature on the rate of an enzyme-catalysed reaction.
questions.markScheme:
1. Rate increases as temperature increases up to the optimum (1)
2. Because kinetic energy increases so more frequent enzyme-substrate collisions (1)
3. Above the optimum temperature, rate decreases rapidly (1)
4. Because hydrogen bonds break, the active site changes shape and substrate can no longer bind (denaturation) (1)

questions.text: Explain the role of carrier proteins and ATP in the transport of sodium ions across a cell membrane against a concentration gradient.
questions.markScheme:
1. Active transport / against concentration gradient requires energy (1)
2. ATP is hydrolysed by ATPase on the carrier protein (1)
3. This causes the carrier protein to change shape, moving sodium ions from low to high concentration (1)

questions.text: Describe the sequence of events during the cell cycle that ensures each daughter cell receives an exact copy of the DNA.
questions.markScheme:
1. During S phase / interphase, DNA replicates by semi-conservative replication (1)
2. Each chromosome consists of two identical sister chromatids joined at the centromere (1)
3. During mitosis, spindle fibres attach to centromeres and pull chromatids to opposite poles (1)
4. Cytokinesis divides the cytoplasm producing two genetically identical daughter cells (1)

questions.text: Evaluate the evidence for the role of carbon dioxide concentration as a limiting factor in photosynthesis. Include in your answer how this knowledge can be applied in commercial glasshouse production.
questions.markScheme:
1. CO2 is required as a substrate in the Calvin cycle / light-independent reaction (1)
2. At low CO2, increasing concentration increases the rate of carbon fixation by RuBisCO (1)
3. Above a certain concentration, another factor becomes limiting (e.g. light intensity, temperature) so rate plateaus (1)
4. In glasshouses, CO2 enrichment (to ~0.1%) increases crop yield (1)
5. Must be balanced against cost of CO2 supply and other limiting factors (1)
6. Evidence from controlled experiments shows clear correlation but commercial application must consider economic viability (1)
